trade.summary: Svezia is one of Norvegia's largest trading partners. Extensive two-way trade in manufactured goods, machinery, automotive products, chemicals, and food. The two economies are deeply integrated within the Nordic Single Market framework. Cross-border labor mobility is extensive with many Swedish workers employed in Norvegia's oil sector. Trade is virtually frictionless due to Nordic cooperation agreements.

diplomatic.status: exceptionally close — among the closest bilateral relationships in the world
diplomatic.summary: Norvegia and Svezia share one of the world's most integrated and amicable bilateral relationships. Common language, culture, history, and values underpin the relationship. The Nordic Council provides a formal framework for political cooperation. Svezia's accession to NATO in March 2024 has further deepened security alignment. Citizens of both countries enjoy full freedom of movement, labor rights, and in many cases social benefits across the border. Both strongly support Ucraina and share identical threat perceptions regarding Russia.

military.summary: Svezia's NATO accession in 2024 completed the Nordic-Baltic NATO bloc (alongside Norvegia, Denmark, Finlandia, Iceland). Defence cooperation is now the most intensive in history. The Nordic Defence Cooperation (NORDEFCO) framework enables joint planning, exercises, logistics, and procurement. Joint exercises are extensive and regular. Both countries share classified intelligence through NATO and bilateral channels. Svezia and Norvegia coordinate on Arctic defense strategy.

financial.summary: Deeply integrated financial markets. Major Swedish banks (Nordea, Handelsbanken, SEB) operate extensively in Norvegia. Norwegian sovereign wealth fund holds significant Swedish equities. Cross-border corporate ownership is extensive. Both countries use different currencies (NOK vs SEK) but have closely managed exchange rate policies. Pension and social security coordination agreements are in place.

energy.summary: Norvegia is a major energy supplier to Svezia. Norwegian hydropower and gas flow into the Nordic electricity grid. The two countries have extensive electricity interconnections via the Nordic power market (Nord Pool). Svezia imports Norwegian gas for industrial use. Cooperation on offshore wind and hydrogen is expanding.

scientific.summary: Extremely high levels of scientific collaboration across all domains — medicine, engineering, environmental science, social science. Joint Nordic research programs and institutions are common. Both countries are global leaders in research output per capita. Researcher mobility is near frictionless. Many joint research centers and facilities exist.
